title: Sour Flaky Thai Fish Curry
description: A bright, tangy Thai fish curry that pairs flaky white fish with a zesty gherkin relish, all in a light coconut milk base.

ingredients.name: Gherkin relish
ingredients.notes: 1 tbsp chopped gherkins, 1 tsp rice vinegar, 1/4 tsp sugar, pinch black pepper

instructions: Pat the fish fillets dry and season lightly with salt and pepper; set aside.
In a saucepan, combine coconut milk, fish sauce, lime juice, tamarind paste, cumin, and chili flakes. Warm over medium heat until a gentle simmer, about 3‑4 minutes.
Add the sliced mushrooms and sauté for 5 minutes until tender.
Carefully place the fish fillets into the broth, cover, and simmer for 5‑7 minutes, or until the fish flakes easily and reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
While the fish cooks, whisk together the gherkin relish ingredients in a small bowl.
Remove the curry from heat; stir in fresh herbs and lime zest.
Plate the fish and spoon the curry sauce over it, then top with a sprinkle of gherkin relish.
Serve immediately with steamed jasmine rice or cauliflower rice if desired.
